quizQuestion:
{___} dije ayer.
(I told him/her it yesterday.)
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: le lo → se lo
explanation: se lo — when indirect object le or les precedes direct object lo/la/los/las, le/les changes to se. Rule: le/les + lo/la/los/las → se + lo/la/los/las. se lo dije = I told it to him/her. Never: *le lo dije.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} has explicado?
(Have you explained it to him/her?)
sentenceTranslation: Have you explained it to him/her?
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: se lo (3rd person IO)
explanation: se lo — indirect object le (to him/her) + direct object lo (it) → se lo. ¿Se lo has explicado? = Have you explained it to him/her? Both pronouns come before the conjugated verb haber.

quizQuestion:
{___} mandé por correo.
(I sent it to him/her by mail.)
sentenceTranslation: I sent it to him/her by mail.
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: se la (feminine DO)
explanation: se la — le (to him/her) + la (it, feminine) → se la. The direct object pronoun (lo/la/los/las) agrees in gender with the noun it replaces. La carta (f) → la. Se la mandé = I sent it (the letter, f) to him/her.

quizQuestion:
{___} presté ayer.
(I lent them to him/her yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: I lent them to him/her yesterday.
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: se los (plural masculine DO)
explanation: se los — le (to him/her) + los (them, masc. plural) → se los. Los libros (m pl) → los. Se los presté = I lent them (the books) to him/her. Plural direct objects: se los (masc), se las (fem).

quizQuestion:
{___} traje de vacaciones.
(I brought them to him/her from vacation.)
sentenceTranslation: I brought them to him/her from vacation.
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: se las (plural feminine DO)
explanation: se las — le (to him/her) + las (them, fem. plural) → se las. Las fotos (f pl) → las. Se las traje = I brought them (the photos, f pl) to him/her.

quizQuestion:
{___} dijo mi madre.
(My mother told me it.)
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: me lo (1st person IO)
explanation: me lo — indirect object me (to me) + direct object lo (it) → me lo. When 1st/2nd person is the indirect object, no change: me lo, te lo, nos lo (unlike 3rd person where le/les → se). Me lo dijo = she told it to me.

quizQuestion:
¿{___} puedes explicar?
(Can you explain it to me?)
sentenceTranslation: Can you explain it to me?
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: me lo before conjugated verb
explanation: me lo — both pronouns come before the conjugated verb puedes. Order: IO (me/te/se/nos) + DO (lo/la/los/las). ¿Me lo puedes explicar? is equivalent to ¿Puedes explicármelo? (attached to infinitive).

quizQuestion:
{___} mandó ayer.
(He/she sent it to me yesterday.)
sentenceTranslation: He/she sent it to me yesterday.
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: me la (feminine DO)
explanation: me la — me (to me) + la (it, feminine). La invitación (f) → la. Me la mandó = he/she sent it (the invitation, f) to me.

grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: te lo (2nd person IO)
explanation: te lo — te (to you) + lo (it) → te lo. No change needed for 2nd person: te + lo = te lo (unlike 3rd person le lo → se lo). Te lo juro is an emotional fixed expression.

grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: te lo (before conjugated verb)
explanation: te lo — te (to you) + lo (it). Ya te lo dije = I already told you (it). This is an extremely common spoken Spanish expression. Pronouns come before the conjugated verb dije.

quizQuestion:
{___} enviaré mañana.
(I'll send it to you tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: I'll send it to you tomorrow.
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: te la (feminine DO + future)
explanation: te la — te (to you) + la (it, feminine). With future tense: Te la enviaré = I'll send it (e.g., la información, f) to you tomorrow. Pronouns always stay before conjugated verb, even in future.

quizQuestion:
{___} explicó el profesor.
(The teacher explained it to us.)
sentenceTranslation: The teacher explained it to us.
grammarPointLabel: double object pronoun: nos lo (1st person plural IO)
explanation: nos lo — nos (to us) + lo (it). Nos lo explicó = he/she explained it to us. 1st person plural: nos lo, nos la, nos los, nos las (no change, unlike 3rd person se).

quizQuestion:
Quiero {___} ahora.
(I want to give it to you now.)
sentenceTranslation: I want to give it to you now.
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to infinitive → dártelo
explanation: dártelo — pronouns can attach to infinitives: dar + te + lo = dártelo. Or they can go before quiero: Te lo quiero dar. Both are correct. With attachment, accent on dar: dár-te-lo. IO before DO: te (IO) + lo (DO).

quizQuestion:
Necesito {___} hoy.
(I need to send it to him/her today.)
sentenceTranslation: I need to send it to him/her today.
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to infinitive → mandárselo
explanation: mandárselo — mandar + se + lo = mandárselo. When pronouns attach to infinitive: accent mark added on the stressed syllable of the verb. le + lo → se before attaching. Alternative: necesito sElo mandar.

quizQuestion:
{___}, por favor.
(Give it to me, please.)
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to affirmative imperative → dámelo
explanation: dámelo — da (tú imperative of dar) + me + lo = dámelo. Pronouns must attach to affirmative imperatives. Accent on the imperative stem: dÁme-lo. Never *me lo da (imperative — would be present indicative). Negative: no me lo des.

quizQuestion:
{___} al profesor.
(Give it to the teacher.)
grammarPointLabel: pronoun placement: attached to usted affirmative imperative → déselo
explanation: déselo — dé (usted imperative of dar) + se + lo = déselo. Usted imperatives also attach pronouns: dÉse-lo. le al profesor → se (because before lo). So: dé + se + lo = déselo. Formal instruction.
